title: Exact Finite Probability Tables

solver_reuse.target: exact finite probability QF_LRA/Farkas regression seeds
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact-rational probability conflicts for finite normalization, conditioning, Bayes posterior, independence equations, and finite distribution distance: a fair coin total is forced to 1 and 3/2, a rain/late table forces P(late | rain)=1/3 but the bad row asserts 1/2, diagnostic-test odds force posterior 2/13 but the bad row asserts 1/5, a four-atom table forces independent heads/red joint mass 1/4 while the bad row asserts 1/3, and a three-atom distribution comparison forces total variation 1/6 while the bad row asserts 1/4.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional finite probability rows only when they add distinct exact-rational mass, conditioning, independence, Bayes-rule, distribution-distance, or finite-process solver pressure; keep continuous distributions, sampling claims, and numerical approximation guarantees in separate horizon lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode finite probability mass constraints as deterministic QF_LRA formulas.
Replay probability-table SAT witnesses through Axeyum model evaluation.
Route impossible normalization, nonnegativity, conditioning, Bayes-rule, independence, or finite distribution-distance constraints through QF_LRA/Farkas certificates.
Add generated finite probability tables once at least two packs need shared table helpers.
Keep continuous distributions and sampling-based claims out of proof status until a separate numerical-honesty track exists.
